title: Writing - Part 1: Renewable Energy
description: Your class has been discussing the transition to renewable energy and the challenges involved.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the challenges in your notes. You should explain which challenge you consider most difficult to overcome, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Installing solar panels and wind turbines requires significant upfront investment that many countries cannot afford.
Renewable sources like solar and wind are intermittent, so efficient battery storage technology is essential.
Fossil fuel industries have powerful political influence and often resist policies that threaten their profits.

structure.introduction: Introduce the need for a transition to renewable energy and the obstacles involved.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first challenge and its impact on the energy transition.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second challenge and argue which is harder to overcome.
structure.conclusion: Give your final view on how to accelerate the transition to clean energy.

content.modelAnswer:
The transition to renewable energy is widely recognised as essential for addressing climate change, but it faces significant challenges. Two of the most important are the high initial cost of infrastructure and political resistance from fossil fuel interests.

Building the solar farms, wind turbines and transmission networks needed to power entire economies requires enormous upfront investment. While the long-term savings are clear, many governments struggle to secure financing, particularly in developing nations. This financial barrier slows the pace of transition at a time when speed is critical.

Political resistance, however, may be an even greater obstacle. Fossil fuel companies employ millions of people and contribute vast sums to government revenues. Their lobbying power makes it difficult to pass the strong climate legislation needed to accelerate change, even when public support for clean energy is high.

In conclusion, while cost is a genuine barrier, political resistance is the harder challenge to overcome because it obstructs the very policies that would address all other obstacles. Bold political leadership is ultimately the key to unlocking a clean energy future.
